messages.content: The following passage contains a mistakes in comparative forms. Identify and correct those mistakes.
The passenger in seat 12B received a complimentary meal, while the passengers in the other seats only received a bag of pretzels.

The following passage contains a mistakes in comparative forms. Identify and correct those mistakes.
Internet addiction results in both severe mental impairment and bodily harm.
The passage does not contain any mistakes in comparative forms.

The following passage contains seven mistakes in comparative forms. Identify and correct those mistakes. Internet addiction results in both severe mental impairment and bodily harm. 2For example, people in the old days are lesser(less) likely to suffer from sleep disorder than their counterparts in the past. 3With their mobile devices at hand all day long, people nowadays are exposing themselves in front of the light-emitted screens over a prolonged period of time unknowingly. The light emission may suppress melatonin secretion (sleep-promoting hormone) and consequently interfere with their sleep quality. 4The more worse ones’ sleep quality, the more weaker their immune system. 5According to Smith (2017), the possibility that people with sleep disturbances suffering from brain tumors is three times as higher as those who have good sleep quality. 6More to note, a recent study (Nunan, 2018) stresses that internet addicts, those who use mobile devices for more than eight hours a day, are more likely to experience symptoms such as shaking hands and vomiting once they are trying to withdraw themselves from the virtual world. 7This situation is exactly same as those who are trying to get out of substance abuses. 8Last but not least, with reference to the interview findings from a recent study conducted by the University of Kowloon, among all their 1000 interviewees, those who surf the net for more than six hours a day are at considerably high risk of suffering from cardio-related diseases such as obesity. 9This is mainly due to the fact those people very often lead a sedentary life style and eat mindlessly in front of their mobile devices. 10Being as much inactive as coach potatoes, they are proner to illnesses while comparing with their counterparts.

2. For example, people in the old days are less likely to suffer from sleep disorders than their counterparts in the past.
3. With their mobile devices at hand all day long, people nowadays are exposing themselves in front of the light-emitting screens over a prolonged period of time unknowingly. The light emission may suppress melatonin secretion (sleep-promoting hormone) and consequently interfere with their sleep quality.
4. The worse one's sleep quality, the weaker their immune system.
5. According to Smith (2017), the possibility that people with sleep disturbances suffer from brain tumors is three times higher than those who have good sleep quality.
6. More to note, a recent study (Nunan, 2018) stresses that internet addicts, those who use mobile devices for more than eight hours a day, are more likely to experience symptoms such as shaking hands and vomiting once they try to withdraw themselves from the virtual world.
7. This situation is exactly the same as those faced by individuals trying to get out of substance abuse.
8. Last but not least, with reference to the interview findings from a recent study conducted by the University of Kowloon, among all their 1000 interviewees, those who surf the net for more than six hours a day are at a considerably high risk of suffering from cardio-related diseases such as obesity.
9. This is mainly due to the fact that those people often lead a sedentary lifestyle and eat mindlessly in front of their mobile devices.
10. Being as inactive as couch potatoes, they are more prone to illnesses when compared to their counterparts.

The following passage contains mistakes in subject-verb agreement. Identify and correct the mistakes in []. 1Banknotes and coins are an indispensable part of Hongkongers’ daily lives. 2While cash still dominate in the city, evidence show that mobile payments becomes increasingly common.

3Ambitious entrepreneurs attempt to tap into this gap in the market. 4One of the most preferred non-cash options in Hong Kong are the Octopus Card, which is originally a prepaid card introduced to the public transport system. 5Octopus has recently expanded its payments option to be made available to some taxis in Hong Kong via the O!ePay business app or WeTaxi app.

6With many players entering the game, the local citizens are spoilt for choice when it comes to mobile payments. 7Included in the list is PayMe, Alipay HK, Apple Pay and Google Pay. 8Linking a credit card remain the easiest way to start using a mobile payment app. 9For example, after users add credit cards to their Google Pay app, the transactions made through the app is handled by the credit card companies. 10Interestingly but unsurprisingly, an area where mobile payments really thrive in Hong Kong are P2P fund transfers. 11Statistics reveals a staggering growth of over 900%.

12All in all, the future of a cashless Hong Kong seem bright if both the regulator and fintech players play their cards right.

1. Banknotes and coins are an indispensable part of Hongkongers' daily lives.
2. While cash still dominates in the city, evidence shows that mobile payments are becoming increasingly common.

3. Ambitious entrepreneurs attempt to tap into this gap in the market.
4. One of the most preferred non-cash options in Hong Kong is the Octopus Card, which is originally a prepaid card introduced to the public transport system.
5. Octopus has recently expanded its payment options to be made available to some taxis in Hong Kong via the O!ePay business app or WeTaxi app.

6. With many players entering the game, the local citizens are spoiled for choice when it comes to mobile payments.
7. Included in the list are PayMe, Alipay HK, Apple Pay, and Google Pay.
8. Linking a credit card remains the easiest way to start using a mobile payment app.
9. For example, after users add credit cards to their Google Pay app, the transactions made through the app are handled by the credit card companies.
10. Interestingly but unsurprisingly, an area where mobile payments really thrive in Hong Kong is P2P fund transfers. 
11. Statistics reveal a staggering growth of over 900%.

12. All in all, the future of a cashless Hong Kong seems bright if both the regulator and fintech players play their cards right.

Complete the blank with Fragments / Gerunds and Infinitives / Connectors . 6Sometimes students find it hard to grasp ideas when they are learning using the traditional method. 7Digital simulations and models can help students ______ better understand various disciplines, _____ also get acquainted with the wonders of the modern world. 8________, technology has assisted teachers in explaining many difficult concepts. 9Teachers can prepare their classes in a more strategic manner. 10By including different types of text, activity models, and interactive controls for students.

6. Sometimes students find it hard to grasp ideas when they are learning using the traditional method.
7. Digital simulations and models can help students [to] better understand various disciplines, [and] also get acquainted with the wonders of the modern world.
8. [Furthermore/Moreover/Additionally], technology has assisted teachers in explaining many difficult concepts.
9. Teachers can prepare their classes in a more strategic manner.
10. By including different types of text, activity models, and interactive controls for students.

4)	Which of the following is the most suitable to complete word group #12?
Students will stop (to learn /learning) if there is no effective communication in class.
Students will stop learning if there is no effective communication in class.

5)	Which of the following is the most suitable to complete word group #13?

_______ the emergence of technology, communication has facilitated the whole learning process.

A	Until
B	Unless
C	Since
D	Up till
C	Since

7)	Identify the fragment in Paragraph 1. Please rewrite the sentence to eliminate the fragment. 1:Can you imagine __living_____ without technology? 2Apart from the argument that technology may create addiction. 3Educational technology has been proved to be a really helpful tool. 4Nowadays, students can make the best use of their time since they have full autonomy of it. 5Here are some ways in which technology has made the academic industry better.

The fragment in Paragraph 1 is: "Apart from the argument that technology may create addiction."

To eliminate the fragment, the sentence can be rewritten as:
"Apart from the argument that technology may create addiction, educational technology has been proved to be a really helpful tool."

8)	Identify the fragment in Paragraph 2. Please rewrite the sentence to eliminate the fragment.
__________________________________________________________________
__________________________________________________________________



9)	Identify the fragment in Paragraph 3. Please rewrite the sentence to eliminate the fragment.
__________________________________________________________________
__________________________________________________________________



10)	Identify the fragment in Paragraph 4. Please rewrite the sentence to eliminate the fragment.
__________________________________________________________________
__________________________________________________________________

Digital Simulations and Models
6Sometimes students find it hard to grasp ideas when they are learning using the traditional method. 7Digital simulations and models can help students _not only_____ better understand various disciplines, ___but__ also get acquainted with the wonders of the modern world. 8_In addition_______, technology has assisted teachers in explaining many difficult concepts. 9Teachers can prepare their classes in a more strategic manner. 10By including different types of text, activity models, and interactive controls for students.


Enhanced Communication
11Communication is the key to an effective class. 12Students will stop __learning____ if there is no effective communication in class. 13_Since______ the emergence of technology, communication has facilitated the whole learning process. 14Whether in a traditional or a virtual classroom. 15With online collaboration tools everyone can just form a community where teachers assign projects to their students in real-time and students may ask for clarifications. 16Students may find it much easier to interact with each other on subject related matters.


Advanced Research
17Cloud storage is a useful tool for learning nowadays. 18We can say goodbye to the days when we had to go through piles of books to find a specific reference for assignments and projects. 19With technology, research has been made much easier. 20Since a lot of time is saved during research.  21Students are able to incorporate a lot of information and knowledge in their projects. 22Students prefer __to learn____ with the help of the Internet.


23Technology advances every day. 24It is important for us to keep abreast of the development of technology. 25The idea that “Education is for everyone” seems to be verified with technology’s involvement.

8) The fragment in Paragraph 2 is: "In addition."

To eliminate the fragment, the sentence can be rewritten as: "In addition, technology has assisted teachers in explaining many difficult concepts."

9) The fragment in Paragraph 3 is: "Whether in a traditional or a virtual classroom."

To eliminate the fragment, the sentence can be rewritten as: "Communication is the key to an effective class, whether in a traditional or a virtual classroom."

10) The fragment in Paragraph 4 is: "Since a lot of time is saved during research."

To eliminate the fragment, the sentence can be rewritten as: "Since a lot of time is saved during research, students are able to incorporate a lot of information and knowledge in their projects."

8)	Identify the fragment in Paragraph 2. Please rewrite the sentence to eliminate the fragment.
__________________________________________________________________
__________________________________________________________________



9)	Identify the fragment in Paragraph 3. Please rewrite the sentence to eliminate the fragment.
__________________________________________________________________
__________________________________________________________________



10)	Identify the fragment in Paragraph 4. Please rewrite the sentence to eliminate the fragment.
__________________________________________________________________
__________________________________________________________________

2:Digital Simulations and Models
6Sometimes students find it hard to grasp ideas when they are learning using the traditional method. 7Digital simulations and models can help students _not only_____ better understand various disciplines, ___but__ also get acquainted with the wonders of the modern world. 8_In addition_______, technology has assisted teachers in explaining many difficult concepts. 9Teachers can prepare their classes in a more strategic manner. 10By including different types of text, activity models, and interactive controls for students.


3:Enhanced Communication
11Communication is the key to an effective class. 12Students will stop __learning____ if there is no effective communication in class. 13_Since______ the emergence of technology, communication has facilitated the whole learning process. 14Whether in a traditional or a virtual classroom. 15With online collaboration tools everyone can just form a community where teachers assign projects to their students in real-time and students may ask for clarifications. 16Students may find it much easier to interact with each other on subject related matters.


4:Advanced Research
17Cloud storage is a useful tool for learning nowadays. 18We can say goodbye to the days when we had to go through piles of books to find a specific reference for assignments and projects. 19With technology, research has been made much easier. 20Since a lot of time is saved during research.  21Students are able to incorporate a lot of information and knowledge in their projects. 22Students prefer __to learn____ with the help of the Internet.


23Technology advances every day. 24It is important for us to keep abreast of the development of technology. 25The idea that “Education is for everyone” seems to be verified with technology’s involvement.

8) The fragment in Paragraph 2 is: "In addition."

To eliminate the fragment, the sentence can be rewritten as: "In addition to assisting teachers in explaining many difficult concepts, technology can help students not only better understand various disciplines but also get acquainted with the wonders of the modern world."

9) The fragment in Paragraph 3 is: "Whether in a traditional or a virtual classroom."

To eliminate the fragment, the sentence can be rewritten as: "Communication is the key to an effective class, whether in a traditional or a virtual classroom. With online collaboration tools, everyone can form a community where teachers assign projects to their students in real-time and students may ask for clarifications, making it much easier to interact with each other on subject-related matters."

10) The fragment in Paragraph 4 is: "Since a lot of time is saved during research."

To eliminate the fragment, the sentence can be rewritten as: "With technology, research has been made much easier, as a lot of time is saved during research. Students are able to incorporate a lot of information and knowledge in their projects, and they prefer to learn with the help of the Internet."
